data.description: Server should be written in a language that is efficient processing images, like go or rust. It will need to make API requests to the printful api based on user content. It should also serve the website.
data.title: set up a server

data.body: TYB-6 narrowed the scope: no Printful endpoints in this implementation. TYB-5 plan updated with a header note pointing at TYB-6's HTTP surface as authoritative. Endpoints to ship: static serving, POST /api/render (saves to disk, returns file_id+url), GET /api/files/{hash}.png, /healthz. Printful endpoints deferred to follow-up.

Shipped: Go server with /api/render, /api/files/{hash}.png, /healthz, embedded static site, embedded woff. SVG template uses absolute user units (font-size 320, letter-spacing -25, dy 228) with a per-render computed viewBox width so resvg renders match the browser preview without relying on em resolution. Buy Shirt button + handler (POST /api/render) added to index.html/script.js. Tests passing including golden-file regression for FOO/BAR. Out of scope per TYB-6 narrowing: all Printful endpoints. 5 commits on server-setup. Smoke-tested healthz, render, file fetch, static assets, validation 400s, and missing-file 404.

Reviewed commits ed080cb..765d631 on branch server-setup (5 commits). Files: server/cmd/server/main.go, server/internal/{render,files,httpserver}, server/internal/httpserver/static/* (vendored copies of repo-root assets), server/testdata/golden_foo_bar.png, server/tools/copy-static.sh, plus script.js (Buy Shirt handler).

What I ran:
- `go test ./...` -> ok (golden render, validation table, hash determinism)
- `go build -o bin/server ./cmd/server` -> ok
- Smoke: GET /healthz returns "ok"; POST /api/render {FOO,BAR} returns {file_id, url}; downloaded PNG is `PNG image data, 3600 x 4800, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced`, sha256 matches the committed golden fixture; Cache-Control on /api/files/{hash}.png is `public, max-age=31536000, immutable`; GET / serves the embedded index.html; 11-char text -> 400 with `field=text`; bad hash -> 404; 10 concurrent identical POSTs all return the same file_id (singleflight working); empty inputs default to "THANK YOU".
- Visual check: rendered FOO/BAR PNG shows 7 lines of FOO with line 4 a solid red BAR, hollow outlines on the other 6, "thankyoubag.online" footer. Matches the browser preview.

Plan deviations called out by the implementer (judged):
- font-size 320 instead of 800: mathematically correct mapping of `20em` at the 16px root used by the in-browser SVG. Good call.
- viewBox computed per-render from text length instead of fixed 4096x3200: recovers the dynamic-sizing behaviour the browser does via getBBox, which the original plan acknowledged would be lost. Better than the plan; well-documented in commit f5eec75.

Code quality observations:
- Body size cap (MaxBytesReader 4 KiB), DisallowUnknownFields, panic recover in render, hash regex on file paths, atomic write via tmp+rename. All the safety primitives I'd expect.
- WOFF->SFNT decoder built in-process (woff.go) so the binary stays self-contained; nice.
- httpserver/router.go fails loudly on empty embed (catches forgotten copy-static.sh).
- Mux uses `GET /api/files/` prefix but parseFilePath also validates suffix `.png`; good defence in depth against ../ traversal even though hashPattern already covers it.
- Out-of-scope: zero Printful API code present; only narrative comments referencing the deferred follow-up. Confirms the TYB-6 narrowing.
- Tests are thin but proportional for a prototype: golden file pins pixel output, validation table covers rune-counted maxlength, hash determinism smoke test. No httpserver-level integration test, but the smoke test above covers what it would.

Acceptance criterion 11 was the one gap: Buy Shirt UI showed ack/error states but lacked the in-flight disable the plan called for. Fixed inline in both `script.js` and `server/internal/httpserver/static/script.js`: added a `renderInflight` guard so a second click during a pending request is a no-op, an `is-loading` class on the button (CSS hook for visual feedback if added later), and split 5xx into a generic "Something went wrong" message per the plan.

Verdict: acceptance criteria met with minor fix.
